questionText: A bar chart shows fish food scoops eaten by four animals on a scale marked 0, 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12. The data are goldfish = 8, turtles = 11, crabs = 5, and frogs = 10. Four corrected chart plans are suggested below. Which plan would make the whole bar chart accurate?
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can read and draw bar charts and line graphs
summary: MCQ for Grade 4 Data Display & Interpretation: Bar Charts & Line Graphs, aligned to Students can read and draw bar charts and line graphs.
options: [{"text":"Put turtles at 12 and crabs at 6 because the scale counts by 2.","correct":false},{"text":"Put turtles halfway between 10 and 12, and crabs halfway between 4 and 6.","correct":true},{"text":"Put turtles at 10 and leave crabs at 5 because only one bar was wrong.","correct":false},{"text":"Change the data to turtles = 12 and crabs = 6 so all bars land on marked lines.","correct":false}]
explanation: On a scale counting by 2, numbers that are not marked exactly can still be shown between the marks. Turtles = 11 should be halfway between 10 and 12, and crabs = 5 should be halfway between 4 and 6. So the second plan is the accurate one. Choice 1 rounds both values to the wrong heights. Choice 3 still leaves turtles wrong. Choice 4 changes the data instead of drawing the bars correctly.
generationMetadata: {"questionNumber":"Q3","questionType":"mcq","subject":"Maths","grade":4,"topic":"Data Display & Interpretation","subtopic":"Bar Charts & Line Graphs","learning_objective":"Students can read and draw bar charts and line graphs","focus":"find_the_error"}

questionText: A bar chart shows toy train rides: red = 6, blue = 14, green = 10, yellow = 12. Method A uses a scale counting by 2 and places all bars exactly. Method B uses a scale counting by 5 and places red between 5 and 10, green on 10, yellow between 10 and 15, but blue exactly on 15. Which judgment about the two methods is correct?
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can read and draw bar charts and line graphs
summary: MCQ for Grade 4 Data Display & Interpretation: Bar Charts & Line Graphs, aligned to Students can read and draw bar charts and line graphs.
options: [{"text":"Only Method A is correct, because Method B plots 14 at 15 instead of between 10 and 15.","correct":true},{"text":"Only Method B is correct, because a scale of 5 is always better than a scale of 2.","correct":false},{"text":"Both methods are correct, because 14 is close enough to 15 on a bar chart.","correct":false},{"text":"Neither method is correct, because bar chart scales must count by 1 or 2 only.","correct":false}]
explanation: Method A is correct because all bars are placed at their exact values. In Method B, red at 6 can be shown between 5 and 10, green at 10 can be on 10, and yellow at 12 can be between 10 and 15. But blue = 14 should also be between 10 and 15, not exactly on 15. So only Method A is accurate. The other choices either treat an inexact bar as acceptable or use a false rule about scales.

questionText: A line graph is planned to show sweets sold over four days: Day 1 = 5, Day 2 = 11, Day 3 = 8, Day 4 = 14. Plan A plots all four points in order and joins them. Plan B plots only Day 1 and Day 4 and joins them with one line. Which statement correctly compares the two plans?
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can read and draw bar charts and line graphs
summary: MCQ for Grade 4 Data Display & Interpretation: Bar Charts & Line Graphs, aligned to Students can read and draw bar charts and line graphs.
options: [{"text":"Only Plan A is correct because it shows the rise to Day 2, the drop to Day 3, and the rise to Day 4.","correct":true},{"text":"Only Plan B is correct because the first and last days show the overall change.","correct":false},{"text":"Both plans are correct because they begin and end at the same values.","correct":false},{"text":"Neither plan is correct because sweets sold should be shown only on a bar chart.","correct":false}]
explanation: Plan A is correct because a line graph must show each day's plotted value in order. Here the pattern is up from 5 to 11, down to 8, then up to 14. Plan B misses Day 2 and Day 3, so it hides the drop in the middle and does not match the data correctly. Choice 2 ignores important points. Choice 3 is wrong because same start and end values do not show the full pattern. Choice 4 is wrong because line graphs can show amounts across days.

questionText:

Step 2: Label the categories on one axis and choose a number scale on the other axis.
Step 3: Use a scale of 2 and draw the lollipops bar to 4.

explanation: Step 1 is correct because a bar chart should have a title. Step 2 is correct because categories and a number scale are needed. questionText: A little zoo keeps a line graph of baby pandas seen from Monday to Friday. Monday was 6. Tuesday was 3 more than Monday. Wednesday was 2 fewer than Tuesday. Thursday was the same as Wednesday. The total for all 5 days was 37. Friday was more than Monday but less than Tuesday. Which set of plotted points makes the correct line graph?
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can read and draw bar charts and line graphs
summary: MCQ for Grade 4 Data Display & Interpretation: Bar Charts & Line Graphs, aligned to Students can read and draw bar charts and line graphs.
options: [{"text":"(Mon, 6), (Tue, 9), (Wed, 7), (Thu, 7), (Fri, 10)","correct":false},{"text":"(Mon, 6), (Tue, 9), (Wed, 7), (Thu, 7), (Fri, 8)","correct":true},{"text":"(Mon, 6), (Tue, 8), (Wed, 6), (Thu, 6), (Fri, 13)","correct":false},{"text":"(Mon, 6), (Tue, 9), (Wed, 6), (Thu, 6), (Fri, 12)","correct":false}]
explanation: Start with Monday = 6. Tuesday is 3 more, so Tuesday = 9. Wednesday is 2 fewer than Tuesday, so Wednesday = 7. Thursday is the same as Wednesday, so Thursday = 7. So far the total is 6 + 9 + 7 + 7 = 29. The 5-day total is 37, so Friday = 37 - 29 = 8. This also matches the last clue because 8 is more than Monday's 6 and less than Tuesday's 9. So the correct set of points is (Mon, 6), (Tue, 9), (Wed, 7), (Thu, 7), (Fri, 8). The other options break at least one rule: option 1 gives Friday as 10, which is not less than 9; option 3 gives the wrong values for Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday; option 4 gives the wrong values for Wednesday and Thursday and also makes Friday too large.
